segments.text: All right. Hi everybody. Thank you so much for joining today's presentation.
Navigating the Autodesk AECO Technology Partner Program.
My name is Jeremy and I work on the Autodesk AEC Technology Partner Team.
Our team works with partners that are building or have built integrations to anyone of the Autodesk AECO solutions,
including Autodesk Forma,
AutoCAD, Rabbit, Navis, Works, Civil 3D, and so on.
I've been with Autodesk for going on eight years now.
And I've been on this team the entire time.
It's great to be here with you today and thanks so much for tuning in.
From Agenda Sampoint, I just want to take a few minutes to talk about Autodesk Forma, Autodesk AECO Technology.
And then of course dive into the Autodesk AECO Technology Partner Program,
how you can go and build integrations to Autodesk AECO solutions
and leverage the Autodesk resources available to you through our program.
So just to start and back things up just a little bit and want to talk a little talk about what is Autodesk Forma and how we view the AECO world.
Autodesk Forma is Autodesk AI, NATO, into industry cloud or AECO.
It connects teams, workflows, and data across all phases of the project life cycle in one shared environment.
With connected data and consistent governance as core teams can make smarter decisions faster, anticipate risks early, and deliver better outcomes.
And then you of course can integrate with Autodesk Forma to deliver meaningful integrations to customers
so that they can go and improve outcomes.
Just diving a little bit more into what Forma can do,
customers can condition models, generate takeoff packages, and estimates.
And of course manage projects throughout the entire construction life cycle,
models, issues, RFIs, budgets, change orders, everything and anything that project teams need to go and build on schedule and under budget,
live within Forma.
All of this is underpinned by Forma data management, meaning that information can flow and be used within each solution during the project.
And from an integration standpoint, we have a robust set of APIs.
Again, these are all all these products are underpinned by that Forma data management,
meaning if you go and integrate with Forma data management,
say for instance you're working with the models or the documents.
All of those or all that information is accessible with the many of the Autodesk AEC solutions.
We have APIs available for the desktop tool so it was being rabid, AutoCAD, NavisWork, Civil3D,
and so on for you to go and build plugins for customers to go and use.
And then of course, Autodesk Forma, which we just talked about a little bit,
we have APIs available for you to go and build integrations with individual issues,
RFIs, the budgets, and so on.
You can sync those items back and forth,
you know, make sure that there is attachments on them, get the status updates,
and so on. It's quite a robust set of APIs that you can go and work with and build integrations
that the customers can go and use to deliver meaningful outcomes within their projects.
And okay, so from a technology partner standpoint,
you know, this philosophy and openness and collaboration,
you know, aligns with our approach to the entire AECO product portfolio.
We for years fostered and supported partners as you develop meaningful integrations across all of the AECO solutions.
And we work hard at Autodesk to deliver value to partners through the technology capabilities we offer.
And again, as I talked a little bit about allowing you to go and integrate with our solutions
and our go-to-market efforts help you raise awareness of those integrations that you built.
And by connecting these deep workflows together, we can drive value to customers.
We can help them streamline their processes, automate tasks,
that previously required significant resources and achieve complex operations that were not possible before.
And in turn, this drives value to Autodesk.
As deep integrations and workflow enhance the value of our products,
customers are more interested in buying, using, and as a result, renewing them, which is obviously valuable to us.
You know, there's a couple quick examples that I wanted to show of what others have done.
Gamma AR is a really good example of a partner that has used our integration capabilities.
Gamma AR uses augmented reality to combine the design world with the real world.
They give users the ability to overlay a design model with what the camera detects on a mobile device.
In this case, it's a tablet, but it can also be used on a phone.
And they built several deep integrations with our technology that include giving customers the ability to import 3D models
that are managed in the form of data management directly into Gamma AR.
Project teams on site can overlay those models with in Gamma AR's augmented reality environment
so that they can understand current progress, burst intent.
If an issue is found, for example, it can be sent directly into form a build with all the corresponding visual documentation.
They've also built integration with form a build assets that gives project stakeholders the ability to mark individual rabbit assets, walls, ceiling beams, etc.
As done, or needing tension, that's example that you see in the middle of the screen.
At the green signal signals that the asset is being installed correctly, whereas the red is something that needs attention by teams on site.
There's several customers up there that are using this integration today to help prevent rework by spotting those issues early within the construction process.
MatterPort is a spatial data platform that creates digital twins by turning physical spaces in the structured useful data.
And they're bridging built reality with design intelligence.
They're a strong example of a partner leveraging Autotus Platform APIs at scale.
They built deep integrations across Autotus Forma, Rabbit, and AutoCAD, embedding real world spatial data directly into AECO workloads.
Again, these integrations help customers reduce rework, improve coordination, and help teams keep project moving as a site conditions change.
And all of this wraps up in our AECO Technology Partner Program.
We work with you to amplify your materials both internally and externally.
Have a customer facing website at autotus.com such integrations that gives you the opportunity to showcase all the good work that you're doing with Autotus APIs.
We provide support on your launch announcement.
And so much more, we have a well-wild Go-To-Market Engine that ultimately will help drive a customer awareness, customer adoption, but also educate our internal audience.
So with that, I really appreciate you tuning in here today and I hope to see you soon.
